Finally, going through, we're creating images for this article, and then we'd be publishing directly to their content management system.
And so what's interesting here compared to like using a tool like AirOps is that everything that we've built, right, it lives in our workflow engine, and it lives in code as well.
So if a model changes, if we want to make an update, it's not brittle where we'd have to make all these changes individually across customers.
We can do it at the code layer.
And so this is a great example because if you look at the first step here, you can see.
It's not just like 10 steps to creating this article.

It's hundreds, if not thousands, where we'll take that keyword, we'll go in and find the traffic, we'll look at different search results, we'll look at competitor content, and then we'll use all that to inform the brief that you're seeing.

If you look at the research step, we're going to generate research questions, and then we'll go in and perform deep research on every single one of those questions, verifying it against objective third-party sources.
And then if you look at the front-checking, we'll actually take the draft of the article, extract passages, and then do deep research on every one of those passages.
And if we can't validate the accuracy at a certain confidence interval, we'll actually suggest a rewrite.
And so that gives you just kind of like a glimpse of how deep we go when it comes to creating this content.
And what's cool is that the writer would have the ability to make changes almost in like a cursor type of experience.
As the content is being created, and there's obviously a lot that we do to collaborate with your team, and we stay flexible to the way that you want to be involved in the review.

To me, it's normally two streams, because there's an editorial I would build for, particularly to build the funnel up to make sure that I have enough segmented ICP-ready content that is divided across their buying intent cycle.

And then there's a part of content that I really want to build to basically make us a thought leader, but in a way that basically breaks the mold that you predominantly have in the channel, as in it's not going to be a research report, or it's not going to be an amazing founder story.
It's coming from an angle that has an impact that people have a hook on.
They think, oh, it's interesting to read about, and then they would consider us in that context.
So as an example, a big extermination company in the UK that I used to work with, Rent-A-Kill, they wanted to break into the market.
More kind of like deeper.
And we created PR pieces regarding the rat population in London, which was like a random kind of like thing to do.
It had context on their, obviously on their business, but obviously nobody really kind of like is interested in that besides a journalist that wants to report on the rat population.
And we got 31 placements in A-listed kind of like publications from newspapers to kind of like high traffic kind of like business websites and stuff, which was, you know, it costed us three grand.
To create, but the media value was roughly about 16 million.
It's just this strategic level as in I can create something that builds the funnel, but really the awareness side of things is where I want to get to because we are a category defining business.
That means that people that will engage with our content is a very small part of the market because they have a direct need that they're investigating in.
I want to make sure that the people that are having a need, but not thinking about this type of solution are aware that exists.
I think this is where our total addressable market at the moment sits.
They are aware of this problem, but it's not painful enough yet for them to go out and really go deep diving and researching for it.
And I think this is where the opportunity lies with the thought leadership strategy behind that, and then segmented that by individual market, be it, for example.
And in the U.S., you probably have it divided in regions, and then the U.K., and then, let's say, France.
But to really make an impact there, that we can basically slice it and be more specific on ICP level on how we want to attack those markets and become better at it.

What is the consequence of that is that every AE could be driving a Ferrari because they would be making so much money because our system allows them to win pretty much, you know, 50% of the deals, argument's sake, right?
So I would basically say an article, if you would be an AE making a million a year, what would you spend it on?
A ridiculous kind of like article that people then will basically take out if it's like, who's making a million a year as an AE?
Because you're using GetAccept, you know, some ludicrous kind of like argument, but it gets a little bit of clicks.
gets an interest of the industry, know, kind of like, oh, what are these guys about?
In that level, I take it a bit further, but obviously in that level, I would create interest in terms of sales leaders would pay attention and say, like, it's a ridiculous claim.
Let me read about it.
And even if it's a little bit tongue in cheek, fine.
I'd like that because they have then an awareness of our product.
And then I will.
Then they will use the traditional, let's say, editorial side of things to go more into the pain point solution level side of things.
I think it's great.
Yeah, I think this would be something up our alley in terms of the content that we would create.
What I don't know and would just have to talk to Marcel more about is what we can do from a PR perspective.
Like, how involved could we be?
How much additional access could we get you?
We could absolutely create this kind of content for you at scale, no doubt about it.
I just don't know how much we would get in the weeds of trying to make those connections to different media outlets.
Yeah.
Cool.
Well, I know we're over time here, so I'll let you guys run.
Any final questions before we think about scheduling the next call?
Not from my side.
No, thanks.
It was very insightful.
Yeah, you bet.
Same time next Monday, work okay?
Or even same time this Friday?
Happy dead.
Move at your pace.
I'm good for Monday.
Calle, what's the first best for you?
Yeah, I think I'm good as well.
Yeah, let's lock it in.
Perfect.
Yeah, in terms of the pricing, it's 20k for that phase one strategy sprint.
And then you would have the ability to opt out if you didn't want to move into the 12 month growth execution.
The growth execution, we start pricing there at 15,000 a month.
And for startups, we tend to be at 12,000 per month.
And so that's just something that you guys can think about ahead of the next conversation we have.
But I'll do some prep for that next call.
I'll go deep on those two competitors that you mentioned.
And then I'll come in to talk a little bit more about how we'd work specific to GetAccept with the thought leadership use case you mentioned as well, Bastiaan.
That'd be great.
Amazing.
One question.
The last question on that last note.
Would you think it's more worth comparing us to PandaDoc, Bastiaan, considering...
I think they're somewhat of a stronger player in the content game.
Crumpet is, they're strong on social, but that's not really the use case here.
Or maybe even a sales enablement platform, because I know they have some overlapping functionality.
But yeah, you would know best fashion.
Yeah, would say PandaDuck would be a good one.
I mean, I would say that one, yeah.
It's a one solution product.
We basically do 25% of what they do as a platform.
So they're competitive, but also at the same time, not really, because we're offering a more of a all-in-one platform, while they basically do a one product solution.
Obviously, they're stronger in that than we are.
DocuSign would be another example of that.
Or in Europe, you would talk about OneFlow as an example.
What about Seismic or something like that?
Any of the sales enablement platforms or not really?
I don't think we really...
We come across them once in a while, but not that much.
mean, a lot more often.
And they are direct competitors.
Thank
And the dog, as you say, is more of a niche player, but they are a strong player.
That's interesting.
Okay, cool.
I'm excited to dig into this as a salesperson myself, but I won't waste any more of your time.
Look forward to chatting next Monday, and thanks for keeping us top of mind, Kyle.
Nice to meet you both.
See you later.
Thanks so much, Tyler.
Cheers.
Have a good one.
Cheers.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce GrowthX's AI-powered content marketing services to GetAccept team and explore potential partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GetAccept is seeking to improve content quality and commercial output, particularly in light of a recent content manager departure](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=117.0)
  - [GrowthX offers end-to-end content marketing services, leveraging AI workflows and human expertise to drive organic traffic and thought leadership](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=135.0)
  - [GetAccept is interested in both traditional SEO/content creation and innovative thought leadership/PR approaches to build category awareness](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=912.0)

## Topics

### GetAccept's Content Marketing Needs

  - [Recently lost content manager, creating opportunity to explore new approaches](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=117.0)
  - [Aiming to establish thought leadership in "digital salesroom" category](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=303.0)
  - [Current organic traffic: \~10-15k monthly visitors, seeking significant growth](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1308.0)
  - [Interested in both SEO-driven content and innovative PR/thought leadership pieces](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=912.0)

### GrowthX's Offering

  - [End-to-end content partner: strategy to daily publishing](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=645.0)
  - [Two-phase approach: 8-week strategy sprint ($20k) followed by 12-month growth execution ($12-15k/month)](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1172.0)
  - [Leverages AI workflows with human expertise (managing director, editor, content manager)](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1233.0)
  - [Focus on editorial content, content refresh, and programmatic SEO opportunities](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1270.0)
  - [Custom workflow engine allows for scalable, adaptable content creation](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)

### GrowthX's Differentiators

  - [Founded by Marcel, experienced in building successful content engines (e.g., IBM, HP, Scale AI)](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=724.0)
  - [Team includes former TechCrunch, G2, TripAdvisor, and Fanatics experts](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=837.0)
  - [Proprietary platform with sophisticated AI workflows and human-in-the-loop refinement](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=858.0)
  - [Demonstrated success: 10M+ ARR in just over a year, working with notable clients](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=882.0)

### Content Strategy and Creation Process

  - [Comprehensive strategy sprint: SEO audit, competitor analysis, persona development](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1059.0)
  - [Deep dive into audience fears, uncertainties, and jobs-to-be-done](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1097.0)
  - [Creation of detailed artifacts to inform AI-powered content workflows](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1590.0)
  - [Rigorous fact-checking and expert review processes built into workflows](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1720.0)

### Potential for PR and Thought Leadership

  - [GetAccept expressed interest in innovative PR approaches (e.g., attention-grabbing stories)](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1902.0)
  - [GrowthX open to exploring PR/distribution strategies, potentially during strategy sprint](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1837.0)
  - [Bastiaan emphasized need for both funnel-building content and category-defining thought leadership](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1965.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Schedule follow-up call for next Monday](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=2151.0)
  - [GrowthX to prepare deeper analysis on GetAccept's competitors (PandaDoc, DocuSign, OneFlow)](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=2199.0)
  - [Explore potential for innovative thought leadership content and PR strategies](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1902.0)
  - [GetAccept to consider pricing structure: $20k strategy sprint, $12-15k/month for execution](https://fathom.video/share/rDcJfXGRYLv611U2TApArCMRovH74aNY?tab=summary&timestamp=1172.0)

action_items.description: Review GrowthX pricing - 20k for strategy sprint, 12-15k/mo for 12mo growth execution. Discuss internally
